payload.answer: This is an explicit correction of an existing preference memory. Replace the old fact that Sam from Goldman likes Bill Evans with the corrected fact that Sam from Goldman likes early Coltrane. Do not keep the Bill Evans preference as current.
citations: Existing memory: "Sam from Goldman likes Bill Evans"
Correction: "Sam from Goldman actually likes early Coltrane, not Bill Evans"

entity_resolution.reason: The correction refers to the same surface-form entity, 'Sam from Goldman', so it should resolve to the same person as the existing memory.

details: Identified same subject/entity across existing and correction statements.
Classified as a direct contradiction on the same preference slot.
Prepared only the corrected fact for durable storage and treated the prior fact as superseded.

repair_options: Delete or archive the superseded memory: "Sam from Goldman likes Bill Evans".
Store the corrected memory: "Sam from Goldman likes early Coltrane".
Do not infer a durable dislike of Bill Evans; the correction only establishes that Bill Evans was the wrong preference to store as current.
